NKJV.1: Therefore you are inexcusable, O man, whoever you are who judge, for in whatever you judge another you condemn yourself; for you who judge practice the same things.
NKJV.2: But we know that the judgment of God is according to truth against those who practice such things.
NKJV.3: And do you think this, O man, you who judge those practicing such things, and doing the same, that you will escape the judgment of God?
NKJV.4: Or do you despise the riches of His goodness, forbearance, and longsuffering, not knowing that the goodness of God leads you to repentance?
NKJV.5: But in accordance with your hardness and your impenitent heart you are treasuring up for yourself wrath in the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of God,
NKJV.6: who “will render to each one according to his deeds”:
NKJV.7: eternal life to those who by patient continuance in doing good seek for glory, honor, and immortality;
NKJV.8: but to those who are self-seeking and do not obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness—indignation and wrath,
NKJV.9: tribulation and anguish, on every soul of man who does evil, of the Jew first and also of the Greek;
NKJV.10: but glory, honor, and peace to everyone who works what is good, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
NKJV.11: For there is no partiality with God.
NKJV.12: For as many as have sinned without law will also perish without law, and as many as have sinned in the law will be judged by the law
NKJV.13: (for not the hearers of the law are just in the sight of God, but the doers of the law will be justified;
NKJV.14: for when Gentiles, who do not have the law, by nature do the things in the law, these, although not having the law, are a law to themselves,
NKJV.15: who show the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience also bearing witness, and between themselves their thoughts accusing or else excusing them)
NKJV.16: in the day when God will judge the secrets of men by Jesus Christ, according to my gospel.
NKJV.17: Indeed you are called a Jew, and rest on the law, and make your boast in God,
NKJV.18: and know His will, and approve the things that are excellent, being instructed out of the law,
NKJV.19: and are confident that you yourself are a guide to the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,
NKJV.20: an instructor of the foolish, a teacher of babes, having the form of knowledge and truth in the law.
NKJV.21: You, therefore, who teach another, do you not teach yourself? You who preach that a man should not steal, do you steal?
NKJV.22: You who say, “Do not commit adultery,” do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?
NKJV.23: You who make your boast in the law, do you dishonor God through breaking the law?
NKJV.24: For “the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you,” as it is written.
NKJV.25: For circumcision is indeed profitable if you keep the law; but if you are a breaker of the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
NKJV.26: Therefore, if an uncircumcised man keeps the righteous requirements of the law, will not his uncircumcision be counted as circumcision?
NKJV.27: And will not the physically uncircumcised, if he fulfills the law, judge you who, even with your written code and circumcision, are a transgressor of the law?
NKJV.28: For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly, nor is circumcision that which is outward in the flesh;
NKJV.29: but he is a Jew who is one inwardly; and circumcision is that of the heart, in the Spirit, not in the letter; whose praise is not from men but from God.

ESV.1: Therefore you have no excuse, O man, every one of you who judges. For in passing judgment on another you condemn yourself, because you, the judge, practice the very same things.
ESV.2: We know that the judgment of God rightly falls on those who practice such things.
ESV.3: Do you suppose, O man — you who judge those who practice such things and yet do them yourself — that you will escape the judgment of God?
ESV.4: Or do you presume on the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience, not knowing that God's kindness is meant to lead you to repentance?
ESV.5: But because of your hard and impenitent heart you are storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath when God's righteous judgment will be revealed.
ESV.6: He will render to each one according to his works:
ESV.7: to those who by patience in well-doing seek for glory and honor and immortality, he will give eternal life;
ESV.8: but for those who are self-seeking  and do not obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, there will be wrath and fury.
ESV.9: There will be tribulation and distress for every human being who does evil, the Jew first and also the Greek,
ESV.10: but glory and honor and peace for everyone who does good, the Jew first and also the Greek.
ESV.12: For all who have sinned without the law will also perish without the law, and all who have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.
ESV.13: For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous before God, but the doers of the law who will be justified.
ESV.14: For when Gentiles, who do not have the law, by nature do what the law requires, they are a law to themselves, even though they do not have the law.
ESV.15: They show that the work of the law is written on their hearts, while their conscience also bears witness, and their conflicting thoughts accuse or even excuse them
ESV.16: on that day when, according to my gospel, God judges the secrets of men by Christ Jesus.
ESV.17: But if you call yourself a Jew and rely on the law and boast in God
ESV.18: and know his will and approve what is excellent, because you are instructed from the law;
ESV.19: and if you are sure that you yourself are a guide to the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,
ESV.20: an instructor of the foolish, a teacher of children, having in the law the embodiment of knowledge and truth —
ESV.21: you then who teach others, do you not teach yourself? While you preach against stealing, do you steal?
ESV.22: You who say that one must not commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?
ESV.23: You who boast in the law dishonor God by breaking the law.
ESV.24: For, as it is written, “The name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you.”
ESV.25: For circumcision indeed is of value if you obey the law, but if you break the law, your circumcision becomes uncircumcision.
ESV.26: So, if a man who is uncircumcised keeps the precepts of the law, will not his uncircumcision be regarded  as circumcision?
ESV.27: Then he who is physically  uncircumcised but keeps the law will condemn you who have the written code  and circumcision but break the law.
ESV.28: For no one is a Jew who is merely one outwardly, nor is circumcision outward and physical.
ESV.29: But a Jew is one inwardly, and circumcision is a matter of the heart, by the Spirit, not by the letter. His praise is not from man but from God.

NASB.1: Therefore you have no excuse, everyone of you who passes judgment, for in that which you judge another, you condemn yourself; for you who judge practice the same things.
NASB.2: And we know that the judgment of God rightly falls upon those who practice such things.
NASB.3: But do you suppose this, O man, when you pass judgment on those who practice such things and do the same [yourself], that you will escape the judgment of God?
NASB.4: Or do you think lightly of the riches of His kindness and tolerance and patience, not knowing that the kindness of God leads you to repentance?
NASB.5: But because of your stubbornness and unrepentant heart you are storing up wrath for yourself in the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of God,
NASB.6: who WILL RENDER TO EACH PERSON ACCORDING TO HIS DEEDS:
NASB.7: to those who by perseverance in doing good seek for glory and honor and immortality, eternal life;
NASB.8: but to those who are selfishly ambitious and do not obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, wrath and indignation.
NASB.9: [There will be] tribulation and distress for every soul of man who does evil, of the Jew first and also of the Greek,
NASB.10: but glory and honor and peace to everyone who does good, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
NASB.11: For there is no partiality with God.
NASB.12: For all who have sinned without the Law will also perish without the Law, and all who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law;
NASB.13: for [it is] not the hearers of the Law [who] are just before God, but the doers of the Law will be justified.
NASB.14: For when Gentiles who do not have the Law do instinctively the things of the Law, these, not having the Law, are a law to themselves,
NASB.15: in that they show the work of the Law written in their hearts, their conscience bearing witness and their thoughts alternately accusing or else defending them,
NASB.16: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the secrets of men through Christ Jesus.
NASB.17: But if you bear the name 'Jew ' and rely upon the Law and boast in God,
NASB.18: and know [His] will and approve the things that are essential, being instructed out of the Law,
NASB.19: and are confident that you yourself are a guide to the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,
NASB.20: a corrector of the foolish, a teacher of the immature, having in the Law the embodiment of knowledge and of the truth,
NASB.21: you, therefore, who teach another, do you not teach yourself? You who preach that one shall not steal, do you steal?
NASB.22: You who say that one should not commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?
NASB.23: You who boast in the Law, through your breaking the Law, do you dishonor God?
NASB.24: For 'THE NAME OF GOD IS BLASPHEMED AMONG THE GENTILES BECAUSE OF YOU,' just as it is written.
NASB.25: For indeed circumcision is of value if you practice the Law; but if you are a transgressor of the Law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
NASB.26: So if the uncircumcised man keeps the requirements of the Law, will not his uncircumcision be regarded as circumcision?
NASB.27: And he who is physically uncircumcised, if he keeps the Law, will he not judge you who though having the letter [of the Law] and circumcision are a transgressor of the Law?
NASB.28: For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly, nor is circumcision that which is outward in the flesh.
NASB.29: But he is a Jew who is one inwardly; and circumcision is that which is of the heart, by the Spirit, not by the letter; and his praise is not from men, but from God.

NLT.1: You may think you can condemn such people, but you are just as bad, and you have no excuse! When you say they are wicked and should be punished, you are condemning yourself, for you who judge others do these very same things.
NLT.2: And we know that God, in his justice, will punish anyone who does such things.
NLT.3: Since you judge others for doing these things, why do you think you can avoid God’s judgment when you do the same things?
NLT.4: Don’t you see how wonderfully kind, tolerant, and patient God is with you? Does this mean nothing to you? Can’t you see that his kindness is intended to turn you from your sin?
NLT.5: But because you are stubborn and refuse to turn from your sin, you are storing up terrible punishment for yourself. For a day of anger is coming, when God’s righteous judgment will be revealed.
NLT.6: He will judge everyone according to what they have done.
NLT.7: He will give eternal life to those who keep on doing good, seeking after the glory and honor and immortality that God offers.
NLT.8: But he will pour out his anger and wrath on those who live for themselves, who refuse to obey the truth and instead live lives of wickedness.
NLT.9: There will be trouble and calamity for everyone who keeps on doing what is evil — for the Jew first and also for the Gentile.
NLT.10: But there will be glory and honor and peace from God for all who do good — for the Jew first and also for the Gentile.
NLT.11: For God does not show favoritism.
NLT.12: When the Gentiles sin, they will be destroyed, even though they never had God’s written law. And the Jews, who do have God’s law, will be judged by that law when they fail to obey it.
NLT.13: For merely listening to the law doesn’t make us right with God. It is obeying the law that makes us right in his sight.
NLT.14: Even Gentiles, who do not have God’s written law, show that they know his law when they instinctively obey it, even without having heard it.
NLT.15: They demonstrate that God’s law is written in their hearts, for their own conscience and thoughts either accuse them or tell them they are doing right.
NLT.16: And this is the message I proclaim — that the day is coming when God, through Christ Jesus, will judge everyone’s secret life.
NLT.17: You who call yourselves Jews are relying on God’s law, and you boast about your special relationship with him.
NLT.18: You know what he wants; you know what is right because you have been taught his law.
NLT.19: You are convinced that you are a guide for the blind and a light for people who are lost in darkness.
NLT.20: You think you can instruct the ignorant and teach children the ways of God. For you are certain that God’s law gives you complete knowledge and truth.
NLT.21: Well then, if you teach others, why don’t you teach yourself? You tell others not to steal, but do you steal?
NLT.22: You say it is wrong to commit adultery, but do you commit adultery? You condemn idolatry, but do you use items stolen from pagan temples?
NLT.23: You are so proud of knowing the law, but you dishonor God by breaking it.
NLT.24: No wonder the Scriptures say, “The Gentiles blaspheme the name of God because of you.”
NLT.25: The Jewish ceremony of circumcision has value only if you obey God’s law. But if you don’t obey God’s law, you are no better off than an uncircumcised Gentile.
NLT.26: And if the Gentiles obey God’s law, won’t God declare them to be his own people?
NLT.27: In fact, uncircumcised Gentiles who keep God’s law will condemn you Jews who are circumcised and possess God’s law but don’t obey it.
NLT.28: For you are not a true Jew just because you were born of Jewish parents or because you have gone through the ceremony of circumcision.
NLT.29: No, a true Jew is one whose heart is right with God. And true circumcision is not merely obeying the letter of the law; rather, it is a change of heart produced by the Spirit. And a person with a changed heart seeks praise from God, not from people.

WEB.1: Therefore you are without excuse, O man, whoever you are who judge. For in that which you judge another, you condemn yourself. For you who judge practice the same things.
WEB.2: We know that the judgment of God is according to truth against those who practice such things.
WEB.3: Do you think this, O man who judges those who practice such things, and do the same, that you will escape the judgment of God?
WEB.4: Or do you despise the riches of his goodness, forbearance, and patience, not knowing that the goodness of God leads you to repentance?
WEB.5: But according to your hardness and unrepentant heart you are treasuring up for yourself wrath in the day of wrath, revelation, and of the righteous judgment of God;
WEB.6: who “will pay back to everyone according to their works:”
WEB.7: to those who by perseverance in well-doing seek for glory, honor, and incorruptibility, eternal life;
WEB.8: but to those who are self-seeking, and don’t obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, will be wrath and indignation,
WEB.9: oppression and anguish, on every soul of man who does evil, to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.
WEB.10: But glory, honor, and peace go to every man who does good, to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.
WEB.11: For there is no partiality with God.
WEB.12: For as many as have sinned without law will also perish without the law. As many as have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.
WEB.13: For it isn’t the hearers of the law who are righteous before God, but the doers of the law will be justified
WEB.14: (for when Gentiles who don’t have the law do by nature the things of the law, these, not having the law, are a law to themselves,
WEB.15: in that they show the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience testifying with them, and their thoughts among themselves accusing or else excusing them)
WEB.16: in the day when God will judge the secrets of men, according to my Good News, by Jesus Christ.
WEB.17: Indeed you bear the name of a Jew, and rest on the law, and glory in God,
WEB.18: and know his will, and approve the things that are excellent, being instructed out of the law,
WEB.19: and are confident that you yourself are a guide of the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,
WEB.20: a corrector of the foolish, a teacher of babies, having in the law the form of knowledge and of the truth.
WEB.21: You therefore who teach another, don’t you teach yourself? You who preach that a man shouldn’t steal, do you steal?
WEB.22: You who say a man shouldn’t commit adultery. Do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?
WEB.23: You who glory in the law, through your disobedience of the law do you dishonor God?
WEB.24: For “the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you,”  just as it is written.
WEB.25: For circumcision indeed profits, if you are a doer of the law, but if you are a transgressor of the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
WEB.26: If therefore the uncircumcised keep the ordinances of the law, won’t his uncircumcision be accounted as circumcision?
WEB.27: Won’t the uncircumcision which is by nature, if it fulfills the law, judge you, who with the letter and circumcision are a transgressor of the law?
WEB.28: For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly, neither is that circumcision which is outward in the flesh;
WEB.29: but he is a Jew who is one inwardly, and circumcision is that of the heart, in the spirit not in the letter; whose praise is not from men, but from God.

CSB17.1: Therefore, every one of you <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> who judges is without excuse. <sup>ⓐ</sup> For when you judge another, <sup>ⓑ</sup> you condemn yourself, since you, the judge, do the same things.
CSB17.2: We know that God’s judgment on those who do such things is based on the truth.
CSB17.3: Do you really think — anyone of you who judges those who do such things yet do the same — that you will escape God’s judgment?
CSB17.4: Or do you despise the riches of his kindness, <sup>ⓒ</sup> restraint, <sup>ⓓ</sup> and patience, <sup>ⓔ</sup> not recognizing <sup>[2]</sup> that God’s kindness <sup>ⓕ</sup> is intended to lead you to repentance?
CSB17.5: Because of your hardened and unrepentant heart you are storing up wrath <sup>ⓖ</sup> for yourself in the day of wrath, <sup>ⓗ</sup> when God’s righteous judgment is revealed.
CSB17.6: He will repay each one according to his works: [3] ⓘ
CSB17.7: eternal life <sup>ⓙ</sup> to those who by persistence in doing good <sup>ⓚ</sup> seek glory, honor, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and immortality; <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.8: but wrath and anger to those who are self-seeking <sup>ⓝ</sup> and disobey the truth <sup>ⓐ</sup> while obeying unrighteousness.
CSB17.9: There will be affliction and distress <sup>ⓑ</sup> for every human being who does evil, first to the Jew, and also to the Greek; <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.10: but glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, first to the Jew, and also to the Greek.
CSB17.11: For there is no favoritism with God. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.12: All who sin without the law <sup>ⓔ</sup> will also perish without the law, and all who sin under <sup>[4]</sup> the law will be judged by the law.
CSB17.13: For the hearers of the law <sup>ⓕ</sup> are not righteous before God, but the doers of the law will be justified. <sup>[5]</sup>
CSB17.14: So, when Gentiles, who do not by nature have the law, <sup>ⓖ</sup> do <sup>[6]</sup> what the law demands, they are a law to themselves even though they do not have the law.
CSB17.15: They show that the work of the law <sup>[7]</sup> is written on their hearts. <sup>ⓗ</sup> Their consciences confirm this. Their competing thoughts either accuse or even excuse them <sup>[8]</sup>
CSB17.16: on the day when God judges <sup>ⓘ</sup> what people have kept secret, according to my gospel through Christ Jesus. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.17: Now if <sup>[9]</sup> you call yourself a Jew, and rely on the law, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and boast in God,
CSB17.18: and know his will, and approve the things that are superior, <sup>ⓛ</sup> being instructed from the law,
CSB17.19: and if you are convinced that you are a guide for the blind, a light to those in darkness,
CSB17.20: an instructor of the ignorant, a teacher of the immature, having the embodiment of knowledge and truth <sup>ⓜ</sup> in the law —
CSB17.21: you then, who teach another, <sup>ⓝ</sup> don’t you teach yourself? You who preach, “You must not steal” — do you steal?
CSB17.22: You who say, “You must not commit adultery” — do you commit adultery? You who detest idols, do you rob their temples? <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.23: You who boast in the law, <sup>ⓑ</sup> do you dishonor God by breaking the law?
CSB17.24: For, as it is written: The name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you. [10] ⓒ
CSB17.25: Circumcision benefits you if you observe the law, but if you are a lawbreaker, your circumcision has become uncircumcision. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.26: So if an uncircumcised <sup>ⓔ</sup> man keeps the law’s requirements, <sup>ⓕ</sup> will not his uncircumcision be counted as circumcision?
CSB17.27: A man who is physically uncircumcised, but who keeps the law, will judge you <sup>ⓖ</sup> who are a lawbreaker in spite of having the letter of the law and circumcision.
CSB17.28: For a person is not a Jew who is one outwardly, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and true circumcision is not something visible in the flesh.
CSB17.29: On the contrary, a person is a Jew who is one inwardly, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and circumcision is of the heart — by the Spirit, not the letter. <sup>[11]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> That person’s praise is not from people but from God. <sup>ⓚ</sup>

AMP.1: Therefore you have no excuse or justification, everyone of you who [hypocritically] judges and condemns others; for in passing judgment on another person, you condemn yourself, because you who judge [from a position of arrogance or self-righteousness] are habitually practicing the very same things [which you denounce].
AMP.2: And we know that the judgment of God falls justly and in accordance with truth on those who practice such things.
AMP.3: But do you think this, O man, when you judge and condemn those who practice such things, and yet do the same yourself, that you will escape God's judgment and elude His verdict?
AMP.4: Or do you have no regard for the wealth of His kindness and tolerance and patience [in withholding His wrath]? Are you [actually] unaware or ignorant [of the fact] that God's kindness leads you to repentance [that is, to change your inner self, your old way of thinking—seek His purpose for your life]?
AMP.5: But because of your callous stubbornness and unrepentant heart you are [deliberately] storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath when God's righteous judgment will be revealed.
AMP.6: He will pay back to each person according to his deeds [justly, as his deeds deserve]:
AMP.7: to those who by persistence in doing good seek [unseen but certain heavenly] glory, honor, and immortality, [He will give the gift of] eternal life.
AMP.8: But for those who are selfishly ambitious and self-seeking and disobedient to the truth but responsive to wickedness, [there will be] wrath and indignation.
AMP.9: There will be tribulation and anguish [torturing confinement] for every human soul who does [or permits] evil, to the Jew first and also to the Greek,
AMP.10: but glory and honor and inner peace [will be given] to everyone who habitually does good, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
AMP.11: For God shows no partiality [no arbitrary favoritism; with Him one person is not more important than another].
AMP.12: For all who have sinned without the Law will also perish without [regard to] the Law, and all who have sinned under the Law will be judged and condemned by the Law.
AMP.13: For it is not those who merely hear the Law [as it is read aloud] who are just or righteous before God, but it is those who [actually] obey the Law who will be justified [pronounced free of the guilt of sin and declared acceptable to Him].
AMP.14: When Gentiles, who do not have the Law [since it was given only to Jews], do instinctively the things the Law requires [guided only by their conscience], they are a law to themselves, though they do not have the Law.
AMP.15: They show that the essential requirements of the Law are written in their hearts; and their conscience [their sense of right and wrong, their moral choices] bearing witness and their thoughts alternately accusing or perhaps defending them
AMP.16: on that day when, as my gospel proclaims, God will judge the secrets [all the hidden thoughts and concealed sins] of men through Christ Jesus.
AMP.17: But if you bear the name "Jew" and rely on the Law [for your salvation] and boast in [your special relationship to] God,
AMP.18: and [if you claim to] know His will and approve the things that are essential or have a sense of what is excellent, based on your instruction from the Law,
AMP.19: and [if you] are confident that you are a [qualified] guide to the blind [those untaught in theology], a light to those who are in darkness,
AMP.20: and [that you are] a corrector of the foolish, a teacher of the [spiritually] childish, having in the Law the embodiment of knowledge and of the truth—
AMP.21: well then, you who teach others, do you not teach yourself? You who preach against stealing, do you steal [in ways that are discrete, but just as sinful]?
AMP.22: You who say that one must not commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who detest idols, do you rob [pagan] temples [of valuable idols and offerings]?
AMP.23: You who boast in the Law, do you [repeatedly] dishonor God by breaking the Law?
AMP.24: For, "the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you," just as it is written [in Scripture].
AMP.25: Circumcision [the sign of the covenant of Abraham] is indeed of value if you practice the Law; but if you habitually break the Law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision [it is meaningless in God's sight].
AMP.26: So if the uncircumcised man keeps the requirements of the Law, will not his uncircumcision be regarded [by God] as circumcision?
AMP.27: Then he who is physically uncircumcised but keeps [the spirit of] the Law will judge you who, even though you have the written code and circumcision, break the Law.
AMP.28: For he is not a [real] Jew who is only one outwardly, nor is [true] circumcision something external and physical.
AMP.29: But he is a Jew who is one inwardly; and [true] circumcision is circumcision of the heart, by the Spirit, not by [the fulfillment of] the letter [of the Law]. His praise is not from men, but from God.

MSG.1: Those people are on a dark spiral downward. But if you think that leaves you on the high ground where you can point your finger at others, think again. Every time you criticize someone, you condemn yourself. It takes one to know one. Judgmental criticism of others is a well-known way of escaping detection in your own crimes and misdemeanors.
MSG.2: But God isn't so easily diverted. He sees right through all such smoke screens and holds you to what you've done.
MSG.3: You didn't think, did you, that just by pointing your finger at others you would distract God from seeing all your misdoings and from coming down on you hard?
MSG.4: Or did you think that because he's such a nice God, he'd let you off the hook? Better think this one through from the beginning. God is kind, but he's not soft. In kindness he takes us firmly by the hand and leads us into a radical life-change.
MSG.5: You're not getting by with anything. Every refusal and avoidance of God adds fuel to the fire. The day is coming when it's going to blaze hot and high, God's fiery and righteous judgment.
MSG.6: Make no mistake: In the end you get what's coming to you—
MSG.7: Real Life for those who work on God's side,
MSG.8: but to those who insist on getting their own way and take the path of least resistance, Fire!
MSG.9: If you go against the grain, you get splinters, regardless of which neighborhood you're from, what your parents taught you, what schools you attended.
MSG.10: But if you embrace the way God does things, there are wonderful payoffs, again without regard to where you are from or how you were brought up.
MSG.11: Being a Jew won't give you an automatic stamp of approval. God pays no attention to what others say (or what you think) about you. He makes up his own mind.
MSG.12: If you sin without knowing what you're doing, God takes that into account. But if you sin knowing full well what you're doing, that's a different story entirely.
MSG.13: Merely hearing God's law is a waste of your time if you don't do what he commands. Doing, not hearing, is what makes the difference with God.
MSG.14: When outsiders who have never heard of God's law follow it more or less by instinct, they confirm its truth by their obedience.
MSG.15: They show that God's law is not something alien, imposed on us from without, but woven into the very fabric of our creation. There is something deep within them that echoes God's yes and no, right and wrong.
MSG.16: Their response to God's yes and no will become public knowledge on the day God makes his final decision about every man and woman. The Message from God that I proclaim through Jesus Christ takes into account all these differences.
MSG.17: If you're brought up Jewish, don't assume that you can lean back in the arms of your religion and take it easy, feeling smug because you're an insider to God's revelation,
MSG.18: a connoisseur of the best things of God, informed on the latest doctrines!
MSG.19: I have a special word of caution for you who are sure that you have it all together yourselves and, because you know God's revealed Word inside and out,
MSG.20: feel qualified to guide others through their blind alleys and dark nights and confused emotions to God.
MSG.21: While you are guiding others, who is going to guide you? I'm quite serious. While preaching "Don't steal!" are you going to rob people blind? Who would suspect you?
MSG.22: The same with adultery. The same with idolatry.
MSG.23: You can get by with almost anything if you front it with eloquent talk about God and his law.
MSG.24: The line from Scripture, "It's because of you Jews that the outsiders are down on God," shows it's an old problem that isn't going to go away.
MSG.25: Circumcision, the surgical ritual that marks you as a Jew, is great if you live in accord with God's law. But if you don't, it's worse than not being circumcised.
MSG.26: The reverse is also true: The uncircumcised who keep God's ways are as good as the circumcised—
MSG.27: in fact, better. Better to keep God's law uncircumcised than break it circumcised.
MSG.28: Don't you see: It's not the cut of a knife that makes a Jew.
MSG.29: You become a Jew by who you are. It's the mark of God on your heart, not of a knife on your skin, that makes a Jew. And recognition comes from God, not legalistic critics.

NIV.1:
God’s Righteous Judgment
You, therefore, have no excuse, you who pass judgment on someone else, for at whatever point you judge the other, you are condemning yourself, because you who pass judgment do the same things.
NIV.2: Now we know that God’s judgment against those who do such things is based on truth.
NIV.3: So when you, a mere man, pass judgment on them and yet do the same things, do you think you will escape God’s judgment?
NIV.4: Or do you show contempt for the riches of his kindness, tolerance and patience, not realizing that God’s kindness leads you toward repentance?
NIV.5: But because of your stubbornness and your unrepentant heart, you are storing up wrath against yourself for the day of God’s wrath, when his righteous judgment will be revealed.
NIV.6: God “will give to each person according to what he has done.”
NIV.7: To those who by persistence in doing good seek glory, honor and immortality, he will give eternal life.
NIV.8: But for those who are self-seeking and who reject the truth and follow evil, there will be wrath and anger.
NIV.9: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil: first for the Jew, then for the Gentile;
NIV.10: but glory, honor and peace for everyone who does good: first for the Jew, then for the Gentile.
NIV.11: For God does not show favoritism.
NIV.12: All who sin apart from the law will also perish apart from the law, and all who sin under the law will be judged by the law.
NIV.13: For it is not those who hear the law who are righteous in God’s sight, but it is those who obey the law who will be declared righteous.
NIV.14: (Indeed, when Gentiles, who do not have the law, do by nature things required by the law, they are a law for themselves, even though they do not have the law,
NIV.15: since they show that the requirements of the law are written on their hearts, their consciences also bearing witness, and their thoughts now accusing, now even defending them.)
NIV.16: This will take place on the day when God will judge men’s secrets through Jesus Christ, as my gospel declares.
NIV.17:
The Jews and the Law
Now you, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the law and brag about your relationship to God;
NIV.18: if you know his will and approve of what is superior because you are instructed by the law;
NIV.19: if you are convinced that you are a guide for the blind, a light for those who are in the dark,
NIV.20: an instructor of the foolish, a teacher of infants, because you have in the law the embodiment of knowledge and truth—
NIV.21: you, then, who teach others, do you not teach yourself? You who preach against stealing, do you steal?
NIV.22: You who say that people should not commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?
NIV.23: You who brag about the law, do you dishonor God by breaking the law?
NIV.24: As it is written: “God’s name is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you.”
NIV.25: Circumcision has value if you observe the law, but if you break the law, you have become as though you had not been circumcised.
NIV.26: If those who are not circumcised keep the law’s requirements, will they not be regarded as though they were circumcised?
NIV.27: The one who is not circumcised physically and yet obeys the law will condemn you who, even though you have the written code and circumcision, are a lawbreaker.
NIV.28: A man is not a Jew if he is only one outwardly, nor is circumcision merely outward and physical.
NIV.29: No, a man is a Jew if he is one inwardly; and circumcision is circumcision of the heart, by the Spirit, not by the written code. Such a man’s praise is not from men, but from God.

NRSVCE.1: Therefore you have no excuse, whoever you are, when you judge others; for in passing judgment on another you condemn yourself, because you, the judge, are doing the very same things.
NRSVCE.2: You say, “We know that God’s judgment on those who do such things is in accordance with truth.”
NRSVCE.3: Do you imagine, whoever you are, that when you judge those who do such things and yet do them yourself, you will escape the judgment of God?
NRSVCE.4: Or do you despise the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience? Do you not realize that God’s kindness is meant to lead you to repentance?
NRSVCE.5: But by your hard and impenitent heart you are storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath, when God’s righteous judgment will be revealed.
NRSVCE.6: For he will repay according to each one’s deeds:
NRSVCE.7: to those who by patiently doing good seek for glory and honor and immortality, he will give eternal life;
NRSVCE.8: while for those who are self-seeking and who obey not the truth but wickedness, there will be wrath and fury.
NRSVCE.9: There will be anguish and distress for everyone who does evil, the Jew first and also the Greek,
NRSVCE.10: but glory and honor and peace for everyone who does good, the Jew first and also the Greek.
NRSVCE.12: All who have sinned apart from the law will also perish apart from the law, and all who have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.
NRSVCE.13: For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous in God’s sight, but the doers of the law who will be justified.
NRSVCE.14: When Gentiles, who do not possess the law, do instinctively what the law requires, these, though not having the law, are a law to themselves.
NRSVCE.15: They show that what the law requires is written on their hearts, to which their own conscience also bears witness; and their conflicting thoughts will accuse or perhaps excuse them
NRSVCE.16: on the day when, according to my gospel, God, through Jesus Christ, will judge the secret thoughts of all.
NRSVCE.17: But if you call yourself a Jew and rely on the law and boast of your relation to God
NRSVCE.18: and know his will and determine what is best because you are instructed in the law,
NRSVCE.19: and if you are sure that you are a guide to the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,
NRSVCE.20: a corrector of the foolish, a teacher of children, having in the law the embodiment of knowledge and truth,
NRSVCE.21: you, then, that teach others, will you not teach yourself? While you preach against stealing, do you steal?
NRSVCE.22: You that forbid adultery, do you commit adultery? You that abhor idols, do you rob temples?
NRSVCE.23: You that boast in the law, do you dishonor God by breaking the law?
NRSVCE.24: For, as it is written, “The name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you.”
NRSVCE.25: Circumcision indeed is of value if you obey the law; but if you break the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
NRSVCE.26: So, if those who are uncircumcised keep the requirements of the law, will not their uncircumcision be regarded as circumcision?
NRSVCE.27: Then those who are physically uncircumcised but keep the law will condemn you that have the written code and circumcision but break the law.
NRSVCE.28: For a person is not a Jew who is one outwardly, nor is true circumcision something external and physical.
NRSVCE.29: Rather, a person is a Jew who is one inwardly, and real circumcision is a matter of the heart — it is spiritual and not literal. Such a person receives praise not from others but from God.

NET.1: Therefore you are without excuse, whoever you are, when you judge someone else. For on whatever grounds you judge another, you condemn yourself, because you who judge practice the same things.
NET.2: Now we know that God’s judgment is in accordance with truth against those who practice such things.
NET.3: And do you think, whoever you are, when you judge those who practice such things and yet do them yourself, that you will escape God’s judgment?
NET.4: Or do you have contempt for the wealth of his kindness, forbearance, and patience, and yet do not know that God’s kindness leads you to repentance?
NET.5: But because of your stubbornness and your unrepentant heart, you are storing up wrath for yourselves in the day of wrath, when God’s righteous judgment is revealed!
NET.6: He will reward each one according to his works:
NET.7: eternal life to those who by perseverance in good works seek glory and honor and immortality,
NET.8: but wrath and anger to those who live in selfish ambition and do not obey the truth but follow unrighteousness.
NET.9: There will be affliction and distress on everyone who does evil, on the Jew first and also the Greek,
NET.10: but glory and honor and peace for everyone who does good, for the Jew first and also the Greek.
NET.11: For there is no partiality with God.
NET.12: For all who have sinned apart from the law will also perish apart from the law, and all who have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.
NET.13: For it is not those who hear the law who are righteous before God, but those who do the law will be declared righteous.
NET.14: For whenever the Gentiles, who do not have the law, do by nature the things required by the law, these who do not have the law are a law to themselves.
NET.15: They show that the work of the law is written in their hearts, as their conscience bears witness and their conflicting thoughts accuse or else defend them,
NET.16: on the day when God will judge the secrets of human hearts, according to my gospel through Christ Jesus.
NET.17: But if you call yourself a Jew and rely on the law and boast of your relationship to God
NET.18: and know his will and approve the superior things because you receive instruction from the law,
NET.19: and if you are convinced that you yourself are a guide to the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,
NET.20: an educator of the senseless, a teacher of little children, because you have in the law the essential features of knowledge and of the truth —
NET.21: therefore you who teach someone else, do you not teach yourself? You who preach against stealing, do you steal?
NET.22: You who tell others not to commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?
NET.23: You who boast in the law dishonor God by transgressing the law!
NET.24: For just as it is written, “the name of God is being blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you.”
NET.25: For circumcision has its value if you practice the law, but if you break the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
NET.26: Therefore if the uncircumcised man obeys the righteous requirements of the law, will not his uncircumcision be regarded as circumcision?
NET.27: And will not the physically uncircumcised man who keeps the law judge you who, despite the written code and circumcision, transgress the law?
NET.28: For a person is not a Jew who is one outwardly, nor is circumcision something that is outward in the flesh,
NET.29: but someone is a Jew who is one inwardly, and circumcision is of the heart by the Spirit and not by the written code. This person’s praise is not from people but from God.
